title: Fiery Smoked Australian Seafood Grilled-Dumplings
description: Rich, springy, Ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly main course featuring seafood stock and grilled dumplings, served on a fragrant cedar plank.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat (200C).
Soak the cedar plank in water for at least 30 minutes before use.
Clean and prepare the rock lobster and mussels.
In a large bowl, combine the seafood stock, rock lobster, mussels, smoked paprika, brown sugar, ginger, garlic, and lemon juice. Mix well to combine.
Cut the rock lobster into bite-sized pieces and set aside.
Skewer the mussels onto the cedar plank, leaving some space between each mussel.
Place the plank on the grill and cook for 5-7 minutes on each side, or until the mussels are cooked through.
While the mussels are cooking, prepare the dumplings. In a large mixing bowl, combine the rock lobster pieces, seafood stock, and a pinch of salt. Mix well to combine.
Using your hands, shape the mixture into small dumplings, about 2 inches in diameter.
Once the mussels are cooked, place the dumplings on the plank and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until they are lightly browned on the bottom.
Serve the Fiery Smoked Australian Seafood Grilled-Dumplings hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Let the dish rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
